prompt: Non avevo fame: ____ poco prima. (mangiare, io)
explanation: When you say something had already happened before another past moment, Italian uses the past-before-past, the trapassato prossimo. You build it just like the passato prossimo but with the auxiliary in the imperfect: avevo, not ho. The imperfect of avere for io is avevo and the participle of mangiare is mangiato, giving avevo mangiato. Writing ho mangiato would be the passato prossimo, a plain past that loses the earlier-than relationship.

prompt: Sapevo già come finiva il film perché lo ____ due volte. (vedere, io)
explanation: The film had been seen before the moment of knowing the ending, so this is the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of avere plus the participle. For io the imperfect of avere is avevo, and vedere has the irregular participle visto, giving avevo visto. Ho visto would be the passato prossimo and would not place the watching before the knowing.

prompt: All'esame eri tranquillo perché ____ molto. (studiare, tu)
explanation: The studying happened before the calm at the exam, so it takes the trapassato prossimo. Build it from the imperfect of avere plus the participle: for tu the imperfect of avere is avevi and the participle of studiare is studiato, so avevi studiato. Hai studiato is the passato prossimo and drops the before relationship the sentence needs.

prompt: La sera eri stanco perché ____ troppo durante il giorno. (fare, tu)
explanation: The doing came before the tiredness, a past-before-past, so the trapassato prossimo. It is the imperfect of avere plus the participle: avevi for tu, and fare has the irregular participle fatto, giving avevi fatto. Hai fatto would be the passato prossimo, a plain past.

prompt: Marco conosceva già la risposta perché ____ il manuale. (leggere)
explanation: The reading preceded already knowing the answer, so the trapassato prossimo is wanted. It is the imperfect of avere plus the participle: aveva for lui, and leggere has the irregular participle letto, so aveva letto. Ha letto is the passato prossimo and would not set the reading before the knowing. The subject Marco shows the person, so the cue names only the verb.

prompt: I bambini non avevano fame: la mamma ____ loro la merenda poco prima. (preparare)
explanation: The snack was prepared before the children were not hungry, so this is the past-before-past, the trapassato prossimo. Take the imperfect of avere plus the participle: aveva for la mamma (lei) and preparato for preparare, giving aveva preparato. Ha preparato would be the passato prossimo and lose the earlier-than sense.

prompt: Non fui sorpreso: Anna mi ____ tutto il giorno prima. (dire)
explanation: The telling happened the day before the lack of surprise, a past-before-past, so the trapassato prossimo. It is the imperfect of avere plus the participle: aveva for Anna (lei), and dire has the irregular participle detto, so aveva detto. The mi here is an indirect object (to me), so the participle does not change. Ha detto would be a plain passato prossimo.

prompt: Eravamo esausti perché ____ tutto il giorno. (lavorare, noi)
explanation: The working led up to the exhaustion, an action complete before another past point, so the trapassato prossimo. Use the imperfect of avere plus the participle: for noi the imperfect of avere is avevamo and the participle of lavorare is lavorato, giving avevamo lavorato. Abbiamo lavorato is the passato prossimo.

prompt: Arrivammo in orario perché ____ un taxi. (prendere, noi)
explanation: Taking the taxi came before arriving on time, so the past-before-past, the trapassato prossimo. It is the imperfect of avere plus the participle: avevamo for noi, and prendere has the irregular participle preso, so avevamo preso. Abbiamo preso would be a plain passato prossimo.

prompt: L'esame andò bene perché ____ tutto a lezione. (capire, voi)
explanation: The understanding preceded the exam going well, so the trapassato prossimo. Build it from the imperfect of avere plus the participle: for voi the imperfect of avere is avevate and the participle of capire is capito, giving avevate capito. Avete capito is the passato prossimo, a plain past.

prompt: Il professore era contento perché ____ un buon tema. (scrivere, voi)
explanation: The essay was written before the professor was pleased, a past-before-past, so the trapassato prossimo. It is the imperfect of avere plus the participle: avevate for voi, and scrivere has the irregular participle scritto, so avevate scritto. Avete scritto would be the passato prossimo.

prompt: I ragazzi uscirono presto perché ____ i compiti nel pomeriggio. (finire)
explanation: Finishing the homework came before going out, so the action sits in the past-before-past, the trapassato prossimo. Use the imperfect of avere plus the participle: for loro the imperfect of avere is avevano and the participle of finire is finito, giving avevano finito. Hanno finito is the passato prossimo. The subject i ragazzi shows the person, so the cue names only the verb.

prompt: Il negozio era vuoto perché ____ quasi tutto. (vendere, loro)
explanation: Selling almost everything happened before the shop was empty, so the trapassato prossimo. It is the imperfect of avere plus the participle: avevano for loro, and a regular -ere verb forms its participle in -uto, so vendere gives venduto and the whole form is avevano venduto. Hanno venduto would be a plain passato prossimo.

prompt: Paolo non era preoccupato perché ____ alle parole di Maria. (credere)
explanation: Believing Maria's words came before the calm, so this is the past-before-past, the trapassato prossimo. Take the imperfect of avere plus the participle: aveva for Paolo (lui), and credere is a regular -ere verb whose participle is creduto, giving aveva creduto. Ha creduto would be the passato prossimo.

prompt: La mattina ero riposato perché ____ bene. (dormire, io)
explanation: The sleeping came before feeling rested in the morning, so the trapassato prossimo. It is the imperfect of avere plus the participle: avevo for io, and a regular -ire verb forms its participle in -ito, so dormire gives dormito and the form is avevo dormito. Ho dormito would be a plain passato prossimo.

prompt: Non andammo al supermercato perché ____ tutto il giorno prima. (comprare, noi)
explanation: The buying happened the day before, ahead of the decision not to go shopping, so the trapassato prossimo. Use the imperfect of avere plus the participle: avevamo for noi, and a regular -are verb forms its participle in -ato, so comprare gives comprato and the form is avevamo comprato. Abbiamo comprato is the passato prossimo.

prompt: Cercavo le chiavi dappertutto: le ____ in tasca poco prima! (mettere, io)
explanation: The keys had been put in the pocket before the searching, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of avere (avevo) plus the participle. There is also a preceding direct-object pronoun le (standing for le chiavi), and with a preceding direct-object pronoun the participle agrees, a rule carried over from the passato prossimo. Le chiavi are feminine plural, so the participle of mettere becomes messe, giving le avevo messe. The present auxiliary (ho) would make it a passato prossimo, and messo or messa would miss the agreement.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree) and preceding-DOP agreement (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo bucket). The aux markpoint matches the bare auxiliary so the agreement markpoint scores independently.

prompt: La torta? Maria l'____ la sera prima. (fare)
explanation: The cake had been made the evening before, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of avere (aveva for Maria) plus the participle. The elided l' is a preceding direct-object pronoun standing for la torta, and with a preceding direct-object pronoun the participle agrees, the passato prossimo rule inherited. La torta is feminine singular, so fare's irregular participle fatto becomes fatta, giving l'aveva fatta. The present auxiliary (ha) would be a passato prossimo, and fatto would miss the agreement.
examiner_note: Two skills: imperfect auxiliary (this tree) and preceding-DOP agreement (inherited, PP bucket). The aux markpoint matches the bare auxiliary.

prompt: Quando sono arrivato, Marco non c'era più: ____ a casa poco prima. (andare)
explanation: Marco's going home happened before the arrival, so the trapassato prossimo. With essere verbs it is the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For lui the imperfect of essere is era, and andare gives andato, agreeing as masculine singular, so era andato. The present auxiliary (è) would be a passato prossimo, and a non-agreeing ending would miss the agreement.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. The aux markpoint tests imperfect-vs-present, not person, which the prompt's subject scaffolds.

prompt: Quando ho telefonato, Lucia ____ da poco. (partire)
explanation: Lucia had already left a little before the phone call, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For lei the imperfect of essere is era, and partire gives partito, agreeing as feminine singular to partita, so era partita. The present auxiliary (è) would be a passato prossimo, and partito would not agree with the feminine subject.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. The aux markpoint tests imperfect-vs-present, not person, which the prompt's subject scaffolds.

prompt: Noi ragazzi eravamo stanchi perché ____ a piedi. (venire)
explanation: Coming on foot happened before the tiredness, so the trapassato prossimo with essere: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For noi the imperfect of essere is eravamo, and venire gives venuto, agreeing as masculine plural to venuti, so eravamo venuti. Siamo venuti would be a passato prossimo, and a singular ending would miss the agreement. The subject noi ragazzi shows the person, so the cue names only the verb.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. The aux markpoint tests imperfect-vs-present, not person, which the prompt's subject scaffolds.

prompt: Le ragazze non c'erano: ____ poco prima. (uscire)
explanation: The girls had gone out before they were found missing, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For loro the imperfect of essere is erano, and uscire gives uscito, agreeing as feminine plural to uscite, so erano uscite. Sono uscite would be a passato prossimo, and uscito or usciti would miss the agreement with the feminine plural subject.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. prompt: Anna, alla festa sembravi riposata: ____ a casa presto la sera prima? (tornare, tu)

translation.reveal: tornare - to return / to go back

explanation: Returning home came before the party, so the trapassato prossimo with essere: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For tu the imperfect of essere is eri, and tornare gives tornato, agreeing with the feminine subject Anna to tornata, so eri tornata. Sei tornata would be a passato prossimo, and tornato would not agree.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. The aux markpoint tests imperfect-vs-present, not person, which the prompt's subject scaffolds. Subject Anna fixes feminine singular agreement.

prompt: Quando vi ho chiamati, ____ a casa da un'ora, vero? (arrivare, voi)
explanation: Arriving home had happened an hour before the call, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For voi the imperfect of essere is eravate, and arrivare gives arrivato, agreeing as masculine plural to arrivati, so eravate arrivati. Siete arrivati would be a passato prossimo, and a singular or feminine ending would miss the agreement.

prompt: Marco e Lucia non risposero: ____ a dormire poco prima. (andare)
explanation: Marco and Lucia had gone to bed before failing to answer, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For loro the imperfect of essere is erano, and a mixed-gender subject (a man and a woman) takes the masculine plural by default, so andare gives andati and the form is erano andati. Sono andati would be a passato prossimo, and andate would wrongly treat the pair as all feminine.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. The aux markpoint tests imperfect-vs-present, not person, which the prompt's subject scaffolds. Mixed-gender subject resolves to masculine plural.
